CONFIRMED. Schema read in full: LOCKED v1.0.0. Key constraints: (1) signal_system_allowed includes 'hematologic' — all CBC/iron/haematology signals map here; (2) trigger_direction_allowed: high, low, bidirectional, context_dependent — 'both' is obsolete and prohibited; (3) Forbidden root keys: hypotheses, hypothesis_ranking, narrative, rendering, presentation, display, report_layout, ui_hints — must not appear in promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml; (4) Required signal fields: signal_id, research_domain, signal_system, primary_metric, trigger_direction, activation, states, supporting_markers, contradiction_markers, missing_data, confidence, override_rules, evidence, confirmatory_test_refs; (5) Lifecycle/governance fields (runtime_activation_state, medical_review_status, scoring_readiness) go in compile_manifest.yaml only — NOT in PSI.

CONFIRMED. Canonical SSOT biomarker keys relevant to Batch C: hemoglobin ✓, transferrin_saturation ✓, mchc ✓, iron ✓, rbc ✓, hematocrit ✓, neutrophil_pct ✓, lymphocyte_pct ✓, lymphocytes ✓, lymphocytes_abs ✓, neutrophils ✓, neutrophils_abs ✓. NOTE: 'plt' is NOT a canonical SSOT key (SSOT uses 'platelets'), and 'wbc' is NOT a canonical SSOT key. However, both 'plt' and 'wbc' are used as biomarker_ids in P1-11 PSI files and passed PSI validator — this is an accepted existing pattern. The PSI schema does not constrain biomarker_id values against the SSOT. Cursor may follow the same pattern for any Batch C platelet or WBC PSI. No blocker.

changes.finding: The promoted_signal_intelligence_schema_v1.yaml prohibits at root level: hypotheses, hypothesis_ranking, narrative, rendering, presentation, display, report_layout, ui_hints. Investigation spec v3 sources contain rich hypothesis and narrative content that must NOT be carried into PSI. PSI receives signal-layer primitives only: activation, states, supporting_markers, contradiction_markers, missing_data, override_rules, evidence, confirmatory_test_refs. Lifecycle fields (medical_review_status, scoring_readiness, runtime_activation_state) belong in compile_manifest.yaml and sprint report only.

The healthiq-knowledge-bus-medical-intelligence agent rule in .cursor/rules/ was a leakage concern in P1-11. Cursor must confirm at Stage 4A that no tooling files (.cursor/rules/, .vscode/, .codex/, AGENTS.md) are staged or committed to the sprint branch. SOP v1.3.1 non-negotiable: no tooling-file leakage into governed sprint branches.

overall_verdict: HARDENED. All 5 front-matter fields confirmed. All 9 prerequisite files confirmed present on main. P1-11 deferred cluster baseline read directly from manifest. Source specs verified by Python scan and grep: hemoglobin has 0 primary specs (both clusters must be reclassified DEFER_INSUFFICIENT_SOURCE_SUPPORT); transferrin_saturation has 0 primary specs (deferral stands); mchc, plt-clonal, plt-marrow primary specs confirmed — medical review blockers unchanged; iron serum has 4 primary specs — frame authority requires active re-evaluation post Batch B; leukocyte cluster has primary specs confirmed — is an active Batch C candidate requiring evaluation. PSI schema (LOCKED v1.0.0), validator, and P1-11 staged pattern all confirmed. Target path knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/p1_12_batch_c/ is new and non-conflicting. No STOP conditions triggered. Three mandatory flags for Cursor: (1) reclassify hemoglobin clusters as DEFER_INSUFFICIENT_SOURCE_SUPPORT; (2) actively re-evaluate iron_serum_batch3 frame authority post Batch B — do not carry deferral forward; (3) evaluate leukocyte cluster as active Batch C candidate. Cursor may proceed under TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH governance.
